original: Macalester University of St. Paul, Minnesota, beats Mount Senario 17-14
Tom Gregory (11) becomes the youngest person to swim the English Channel
Ferdinand Magellan's Spanish expedition, now led byJuan Sebastián Elcano, returns to Seville without its captain after a three-year epic voyage, becoming the first to circumnavigate the globe
French runner Chantal Langlacé sets the women's 100 km world record in 7:27.22 in Amiens, France
Conjoined twins Benjamin and Patrick Binder are separated at Johns Hopkins Hospital
Strain of bacteria nicknamed "Conan the Bacterium" survives three years attached to the International Space Station in open space
Red Bull's Max Verstappen sets fastest Formula One lap in history with 1:18.792, surpassing Lewis Hamilton's record of 1:18.887, at the Italian Grand Prix
KingCharles IIof England spends a day hiding in an oak tree during his escape after losing the Battle of Worcester
First chord change in 7 years inJohn Cage's "As Slow As Possible" composition is being played for 639 years in Halberstadt, Germany
Russian pilot Belenko defects to Japan in a MiG-25 jet
Crippled Soviet Soyuz TM-5 lands safely with two cosmonauts aboard
Sri Lankan cricket fast bowler Lasith Malinga takes 4 wickets in 4 balls and finishes with 5 for 6 as New Zealand is all out for 88 in a 37-run defeat in the 3rd T-20 International in Pallekele
Wilson Greatbatch, American engineer and pioneering inventor (cardiac pacemaker), born in Buffalo, New York (d. 2011)
Hurricane Irma makes landfall on the Caribbean islands of Barbuda, Sint Maarten, and the British Virgin Islands. Prime Minister Gaston Browne reports 95% of buildings in Barbuda are damaged
Franziska van Almsick swims a women's world record in the 200 m freestyle (1:56.78)
Charles "Buddy" Bolden, African-American jazz cornetist, born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 1931)
Norman Joseph Woodland, American co-inventor of the barcode, born in Atlantic City, New Jersey (d. 2012)[1]
Sergio Aragonés, Spanish-Mexican cartoonist (Groo the Wanderer), born in Sant Mateu, Spain
Richard J. Roberts, English biochemist and molecular biologist, recipient of the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, born in Derby, England
David Bushnell's "Turtle" attacks British ship "Eagle" in New York Bay in the first (failed) submarine attack
First westbound train arrives in San Francisco
Chicago White Stockings beat Detroit Wolverines 26-6 at Lake Front Park, Chicago, setting an MLB record with 18 runs in the 7th inning
Historian Lee Allen says the Cleveland Indians-Washington Senators game is the 100,000th in Major League Baseball history
US College football longest losing streak of 50 games ends for Macalester College (St. Paul)
Douglas Wakiihuri wins a marathon in 2:11.48
Los Angeles County records its highest-ever temperature of 121°F (49.4°C)
Philadelphia Eagles win 34-29 over Green Bay Packers at Corinthians Arena in São Paulo, Brazil, in the first NFL football game in the Southern Hemisphere
US Open Women's Tennis: Aryna Sabalenka of Belarus becomes first woman since Serena Williams (2013-14) to claim back-to-back US titles with a 6-3, 7-6 win over Amanda Anisimova
First Test cricket game in England begins withW. G. Gracescoring 152 on debut against Australia at The Oval
SingerFrank Sinatrabrings surprise guestDean Martinto his appearance onJerry Lewis's 11th Muscular Dystrophy Association telethon, the first time Martin and Lewis share a stage in 20 years
After St. Paul's Cathedral and much of the city burned down over four days, the Great Fire of London is finally extinguished
Margaret Keane, American painter whose husband claimed credit for her work (Big Eyes paintings), born in Nashville, Tennessee (d. 2022)[1]
Carl E. Walz, American NASA astronaut (STS 51, 65, 79), born in Cleveland, Ohio
Michael Winslow, American actor and comedian (Police Academy), born in Spokane, Washington
Cherokee Nation unites and ratifies constitution at Tahlequah, Oklahoma
Baltimore Orioles'Eddie Murrayhits his 500th career home run
Frederick Douglassis the first Black delegate to a national convention in the US
New York Times headline announces American explorerRobert Pearydiscovered the North Pole 5 months earlier (now thought unlikely)
Eric Claptonrecords guitar tracks forGeorge Harrison's Beatles song "While My Guitar Gently Weeps"
August 11, 3114 BBCE is the widely accepted date as the starting point of the Maya/Mesoamerican Long Count Calendar; this date is the corresponding date on the Julian Calendar
First true supermarket, "Piggly Wiggly," is established by Clarence Saunders in Memphis, Tennessee; the grand opening is delayed by construction until the 11th
Irina Solovyova, Russian cosmonaut, born in Tula Oblast, Soviet Union
Henry Diltz, American music photographer (Woodstock; Monterey Pop Festival; album covers), born in Kansas City, Missouri
Susumu Tonegawa, Japanese molecular biologist (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine 1987), born in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan
Siege of Candia on Crete ends with the Venetian fortress surrendering to the Ottomans, the second-longest siege in history (began 1648)
Swedish Admiral Stenbock sails out with a fleet of 66 ships
First lighthouse is built in North America (Boston)
Oberlin Collegiate Institute in Ohio goes co-ed (4 women, 30 men)
Great fire in New York
Southern Pacific railroad line from Los Angeles to San Francisco is completed in California
Saskatchewan (then Regina) Roughriders football club is formed
Charles Paddock wins the 100 and 200 yards at the AAU national senior outdoor track and field championships
Third Women's World Games (track and field competition for women) take place over three days at the Letná Stadium in Prague, Czechoslovakia
Michael Spinks TKO's Steffen Tangstad in the 4th round for the heavyweight boxing title
The name Saint Petersburg is restored to Russia's second largest city, which had been renamed Leningrad in 1924
Africa's first climate summit opens in Nairobi, Kenya, calling for greater climate-related financing from industrialized countries
WolfgangMozart's dramatic opera "La Clemenza di Tito" premieres at the Estates Theatre in Prague
Jack Dempseyknocks out Billy Miske in the third round for the heavyweight boxing title in the first radio broadcast of a prizefight
"Steamboat Round the Bend," a film directed byJohn Fordand starringWill Rogers, is released weeks after Rogers' death
Elvisrecords "White Christmas," "Silent Night," and "Here Comes Santa Claus"
